questions.stem: Look at the dark clouds. I think it ______ soon.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据 'Look at the dark clouds' 和 'soon'（很快）判断，这里是对将来情况的预测，应用一般将来时 will rain。A 是一般现在时，表示经常性动作；B 是现在进行时，表示此刻正在发生；D 是一般过去时。【技巧】will + 动词原形表示将来预测，常伴有 I think/I believe 等表达观点的句式。

questions.stem: ______ you study harder, you ______ better grades next term.
questions.explanation: 【解析】if 引导真实条件句，遵循 '主将从现' 原则：if 从句用一般现在时，主句用一般将来时。句意：如果你更努力学习，下学期你会取得更好的成绩。unless = if not（除非），代入后语义不通：'除非更努力，你会取得好成绩' 逻辑矛盾。B/C 主句缺少 will；D unless 语义不对。【技巧】if + 现，主 + will。看到 if 条件句先锁定 '主将从现'。

questions.stem: The weather in Shenzhen is ______ than that in Beijing in winter.
questions.explanation: 【解析】句中有 than，必须用比较级。warm 是单音节词，比较级直接加 -er，即 warmer。A 是原级；C/D 是最高级，不能和 than 搭配。【技巧】看到 than 就锁定比较级。单音节词加 -er，多音节词加 more，以辅音+y 结尾变 y 为 i 加 -er（happy -> happier）。

questions.stem: ______ a basketball match between Class 1 and Class 2 tomorrow afternoon.
options: C. There is going to have
questions.explanation: 【解析】there be 句型表示 '存在有'，将来时用 There will be 或 There is/are going to be。have 表示 '拥有'，主语通常是人，不能与 there be 混用。A 和 C 错在 there be 和 have 杂糅；D 是 there has，结构错误。【技巧】there be 的将来时：There will be / There is going to be。记住 there be 和 have 不能同时出现。

questions.stem: Hurry up! The bus ______. We need to run!
questions.explanation: 【解析】'Hurry up!' 和 'We need to run!' 表明公交车 '正在来/马上到'。某些表示位移的动词（come, go, leave, arrive）可用现在进行时表示即将发生的将来动作。A 是一般现在时，缺少紧迫感；C 是过去时；D 虽然语法对，但 is coming 更生动地表达 '车来了' 的即时感。【技巧】come/go/leave/arrive 的现在进行时可表将来计划或即将发生的动作。

questions.stem: The underlined word 'rapid' is closest in meaning to ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】rapid = 快速的，与 quick 意思最接近。A(slow) 是反义词；C(careful) 和 D(difficult) 语义不相关。【技巧】rapid 常见于 'rapid growth/change/development'，记忆联想：rap（说唱）+ id = 说唱速度很快。

questions.stem: The ______ of this new medicine is a young scientist from Wuhan.
questions.explanation: 【解析】句意：这种新药的______是一位来自武汉的年轻科学家。空格处需要表示 '人' 的名词。invent(动词，发明)；invention(名词，发明物)；inventor(名词，发明家)；inventive(形容词，有创造力的)。科学家是人，用 inventor。【技巧】-or / -er 结尾常表示 '做某事的人'：inventor, actor, driver, teacher。

questions.stem: After hours of walking, we were all ______ and drank a lot of water.
questions.explanation: 【解析】语境选词。后文 'drank a lot of water'（喝了很多水）暗示前文是 '口渴的'。thirsty = 口渴的。B(hungry) 应该吃东西而非喝水；C(sleepy) 应该睡觉；D(angry) 与喝水无关。【技巧】利用后文动作反推前文状态：drank water -> thirsty，ate food -> hungry。

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a cooking club. I decided to join because I wanted to learn how to make real meals instead of just eating instant noodles at home.

On the first day, the teacher, Chef Wang, told us that cooking is not only about following recipes. 'You also need to use your (9)______ and be creative,' he said. Then he gave each of us some tomatoes and eggs.

I thought it would be easy, but I was wrong. When I put the eggs into the hot oil, they started to stick to the pan. I got nervous and nearly (10)______. Chef Wang came over calmly and showed me the right way to control the heat. 'Cooking is like learning to ride a bike,' he smiled. 'You will fall a few times before you get it right.'

By the end of the class, my tomato and egg dish did not look perfect, but it tasted surprisingly good. My classmates agreed that it was full of character. I felt very (11)______.

Now I go to the cooking club every Wednesday. It has taught me that failure is just the first step to success. As Chef Wang always says, the best meals are made with patience, not just (12)______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】语境推理。后文提到 'be creative'（有创造力），与 creativity 直接呼应的是 imagination（想象力）。senses（感官）、strength（力量）、memory（记忆）均与 'creative' 语义关联较弱。【技巧】完形填空注意同义/近义复现，creative 和 imagination 属于同一语义场。

【解析】语境推理。前文说 '鸡蛋粘锅了，我很紧张'，后文说 'Chef Wang 走过来 calmly 教我控制火候' - 暗示 '我' 差点就放弃了。give up = 放弃。look up（查阅/抬头看）、set off（出发）、come back（回来）均不符合 '紧张+差点' 的情感逻辑。【技巧】利用情感线索：紧张(nervous) -> 负面结果 -> give up，再由他人鼓励继续。

【解析】情感推断。虽然菜 'did not look perfect'（看起来不完美），但 'tasted surprisingly good'（味道出奇地好），而且同学评价 'full of character'（很有特色）。这种正面反馈应该让作者感到 proud（自豪的）。bored（无聊）、worried（担心）、lonely（孤独）均为负面情感，与上下文矛盾。【技巧】注意 but 转折后的情感走向，从 '不完美' 到 '好吃' 再到 '自豪'。
【解析】主旨升华句。前文 'patience'（耐心）是抽象品质，空格处也应是抽象概念，且与烹饪成功相关。skill（技巧）与 patience 并列，共同构成做好一顿饭的关键。salt/sugar 是具体调料，speed（速度）与 Chef Wang 强调的 '耐心' 形成对比而非并列。【技巧】夹叙夹议的完形最后一段常出现主旨升华，注意抽象名词的对仗。

questions.group_passage:
Have you ever wondered why the bicycle is one of the most popular inventions in the world? It is simple, cheap, and good for both our health and the environment.

The first bicycle appeared in Germany in 1817. It was made of wood and had no pedals. The rider had to push it along with his feet. People called it the 'running machine'. About fifty years later, pedals were added to the front wheel, making cycling much easier.

In the late 1800s, the safety bicycle was invented. It had two wheels of the same size, a chain to drive the rear wheel, and rubber tyres. This design is almost the same as the bicycles we ride today.

Today, bicycles are used all over the world. In cities like Amsterdam and Copenhagen, more people travel by bike than by car. Some modern bicycles even have electric motors to help riders climb hills. However, the basic idea has not changed for over a century: two wheels, a frame, and human power.
questions.stem: When did the first bicycle appear?
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节题。原文第二段第一句明确说 'The first bicycle appeared in Germany in 1817.'。A、C、D 均为干扰项，通过改动年份制造混淆。【技巧】细节题直接定位数字信息，注意 1817 与 1807/1867/1877 的区别。

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage about early bicycles?
options: A. They were made of metal.
B. They were easy to ride.
C. Riders had to use their feet to move them.
D. They had rubber tyres from the beginning.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断。原文第二段说 'It was made of wood and had no pedals. The rider had to push it along with his feet.' 由此可推断早期自行车需要骑行者用脚蹬地前进。A 错（是木头不是金属）；B 错（没有踏板，不容易骑）；D 错（橡胶轮胎是 late 1800s 才出现的）。【技巧】推理题要从原文信息推进一步，不能照抄但要有依据。

questions.stem: The underlined word 'pedals' in Paragraph 2 most probably means the parts used to ______.
options: A. make the bicycle move by foot
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测。原文说早期的车 'had no pedals'，骑行者 'had to push it along with his feet'。大约五十年后 'pedals were added... making cycling much easier'。对比可知：没有 pedals -> 用脚推地；有了 pedals -> 骑车更容易。由此推断 pedals 是用脚驱动自行车前进的部件。【技巧】利用对比逻辑：no pedals + feet pushing vs pedals added -> easier，推断 pedals 替代了 'feet pushing' 的功能。

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. How to Ride a Bicycle Safely
B. The History of the Bicycle
D. Why Cycling is Good Exercise
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意。文章按时间顺序介绍了自行车的发展：1817年木制无踏板 -> 五十年后加踏板 -> 19世纪末安全自行车 -> 现代自行车。核心是 '历史演变'。A 只涉及安全骑行；C 只涉及现代城市；D 只涉及锻炼好处，均过于片面。【技巧】主旨题看全文结构 - 时间线叙事通常是 'history of...'。

questions.group_passage:
Staying healthy as a student is not as hard as it sounds. Here are some simple habits that can make a big difference.

First, never skip breakfast. (17)______ Studies show that students who eat breakfast usually perform better in class and feel less tired in the morning.

Second, drink enough water. Many students prefer sugary drinks like cola, but water is the best choice for your body. (18)______ Try to drink at least six to eight glasses every day.

Third, get moving. You do not have to run a marathon. (19)______ Walking to school, riding a bike, or even dancing in your room for fifteen minutes can help keep you fit.

(20)______ When you sleep well, your brain can process what you learned during the day. Aim for eight to nine hours of sleep each night.
options: A. A simple walk after dinner can improve your sleep quality.
B. It gives your brain and body the energy they need to start the day.
C. Even thirty minutes of exercise a day is enough to stay healthy.
D. It helps carry nutrients to your cells and keeps your skin clear.
E. Finally, do not underestimate the power of a good night's sleep.
questions.explanation: 【解析】空格前说 'First, never skip breakfast.'（首先，不要不吃早餐），空格后说 'Studies show that students who eat breakfast usually perform better...'。空格处应解释为什么要吃早餐。B（它给你的大脑和身体提供开始一天所需的能量）完美衔接 - 先说不要跳过，再解释原因，再引用研究。A 谈散步和睡眠；C 谈运动；D 虽然谈水但放在早餐段不对；E 是最后一点。

options: A. A simple walk after dinner can improve your sleep quality.
B. It gives your brain and body the energy they need to start the day.
C. Even thirty minutes of exercise a day is enough to stay healthy.
D. It helps carry nutrients to your cells and keeps your skin clear.
E. Finally, do not underestimate the power of a good night's sleep.
questions.explanation: 【解析】空格前说 'water is the best choice for your body'（水是对身体最好的选择），空格后说 'Try to drink at least six to eight glasses every day'。空格处应解释喝水的好处。D（它帮助把营养输送到细胞，并让你的皮肤保持清爽）是喝水对身体的具体益处。A 谈散步；B 谈早餐；C 谈运动；E 谈睡眠。

options: A. A simple walk after dinner can improve your sleep quality.
B. It gives your brain and body the energy they need to start the day.
C. Even thirty minutes of exercise a day is enough to stay healthy.
D. It helps carry nutrients to your cells and keeps your skin clear.
E. Finally, do not underestimate the power of a good night's sleep.
questions.explanation: 【解析】空格前说 'You do not have to run a marathon.'（你不必跑马拉松），暗示运动量不需要很大。空格后列举了散步、骑车、跳舞等轻度运动。C（即使每天运动三十分钟也足以保持健康）承接 '不必跑马拉松'，说明少量运动即可。A 谈散步和睡眠，放在运动段末尾不如 C 贴切；B/D/E 已用于其他空或主题不符。

options: A. A simple walk after dinner can improve your sleep quality.
B. It gives your brain and body the energy they need to start the day.
C. Even thirty minutes of exercise a day is enough to stay healthy.
D. It helps carry nutrients to your cells and keeps your skin clear.
E. Finally, do not underestimate the power of a good night's sleep.
questions.explanation: 【解析】空格位于最后一段开头，后文整段都在讲睡眠的重要性：'When you sleep well, your brain can process...' 'Aim for eight to nine hours...'。E（最后，不要低估一夜好眠的力量）是段落主题句，引出睡眠话题。Finally 也与前文的 First/Second/Third 形成顺序呼应。A 虽谈睡眠但放在段首不如 E 有力；B/C/D 已用于其他空。
